item.title: Start Believing
item.description: Doubting The doubts I have in myself has always bothered me. And many times the damage is being done without my consiocious ackowledgement. It’s easy to doubt if something will not work out. It’s easy to doubt the path you’re on. It’s easy to doubt if the thing you’re working on is not worth it. What if all the efforts go into waste? Believing Doubting is easy because most creative outputs won’t pan out; there’s a much higher probabilty of you being right if you choose to bet on things failing.

item.title: Changing One's Own Mind
item.description: It’s impossible to be right all the time. The people that never change their minds, either they are geniuses that don’t compromise their ultimate truths when those truths are challenged by the brutal reality, or they are not humble enough to admit they are wrong. Unless you are always right, which is impossible, you should not hold your position when you shouldn’t. It’s okay to be wrong. Just check your ego and think again.

item.title: New Project: Start a Blog or Newsletter?
item.description: There are many ideas I want to work on, but due to the limited time I have, I can only work on one of those ideas (one is about the future of work and the other one is about business bootstrapping). At least for now. It’s quite easy for me to come up with ideas nowadays. Not saying they are always good in the context of building a profitable business, but I do think some of them are good.

item.title: The Imperfect Writing of a Perfectionist
item.description: I am supposed to be writing everyday, for that I have to write for my newsletter project, Valuetal; my personal blog; and just practice more so the readers don’t have to suffer too much. But I am a “perfectionist”, and it’s not such a good thing when it comes to writing daily. Being a perfectionist in theory, means sacrificing quantity for quality. For example, in order to write better, I plan to read some books about writing – in general, how to write better – just to learn how to really do it right.

item.title: The Beauty of the Internet and Products
item.description: Lately, I traveled to Taiwan. Packing has been a big problem for me whenever I travel, especially if I have to stay at the destination location for a longer period of time. I am an avid book reader and rely on books for work a lot. It’s troubling because physical books are heavy and could easily make luggage go overweight; it doesn’t help when I want to bring a small library (I try to limit myself to 15 books now) with me.

item.title: Blogging Again
item.description: This blog is not the first blog I have started. And this post is not the first blog post I have written. In fact, I have written many posts that never see the light of the day. Some of them are buried somewhere in the note or document applications, and some are hidden in my laptop’s directories as markdown files. If not for the imposter syndrome, fear of being imperfect, and lack of conviction I could have started a blog roughly four years ago and the time thereafter.
